description: Stellar-Save chaos experiments for staging environment

experiments.name: Soroban RPC Outage
experiments.description: Block all outbound traffic to the Soroban RPC endpoint for 60 seconds. The backend should degrade gracefully: return 503 with a Retry-After header, and resume automatically when connectivity is restored.
experiments.expectedBehavior: Backend returns HTTP 503 with Retry-After header for contract calls. No panic/crash. Metrics show increased error rate but process remains alive. Recovery within 30s after connectivity restored.
sloAssertions: health endpoint returns 200 (or 503 with valid body) — not a 5xx crash
error logs include structured context (correlationId, service)

experiments.name: Database Latency Injection
experiments.description: Inject 500ms latency on all traffic to the Postgres port (5432) for 90 seconds. API p95 should degrade but not exceed the SLO timeout. No transactions should be lost.
experiments.expectedBehavior: API p95 latency rises proportionally. Requests do not time out below the 5000ms application timeout. Connection pool absorbs burst. Queries complete (slowly) — no data loss.
sloAssertions: application does not kill the Postgres connection pool
latency returns to baseline within 30s after injection removed

experiments.name: Redis Cache Eviction
experiments.description: Flush all Redis keys (FLUSHDB) to simulate a cache eviction / Redis restart. The backend should handle cache misses gracefully and fall back to the database without errors.
experiments.expectedBehavior: Cache miss rate spikes to 100% immediately after flush. Requests fall through to the DB and succeed. No 5xx errors. Cache repopulates within the warm-up period. No user-visible data loss.
sloAssertions: all API endpoints return 2xx after flush
no 5xx errors within 10 requests following flush
cache hit rate returns above 50% within 60s

experiments.name: Worker Process Crash
experiments.description: Send SIGKILL to the backend Node.js process. The process manager (ECS / PM2) should restart it and the service should be healthy within the RTO.
experiments.expectedBehavior: Process restarts within 30 seconds (ECS health check period). All in-flight requests fail with 502 (gateway level). After restart, the service returns 200 on /health and resumes normal operation. No permanent data loss.
sloAssertions: health endpoint returns 200 within 30s of restart
no database corruption — groups and members data intact
in-flight requests fail with 502, not silent data drop
